DECLARATION OF GLENN M. GOFFIN IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT AMY TUCKERS MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CAL. CIV. CODE SECT 3426.4 (TRANSACTION ID # 15243076) FILED BY DEFENDANT TUCKER, AMY AN INDIVIDUAL
LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMY TUCKER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AT THE REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AMY TUCKER'S DEFENDANT AMY TUCKERS MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES PURSUANT TO CAL. CIV. CODE SEC. 3426.4 IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $50,000. DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO FEES PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CIVIL CODE SECTION 3426.4 BECAUSE DEFENDANT WAS THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THE MATTER BROUGHT BY PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM WAS MADE IN BAD FAITH. (CYPRESS SEMICONDUCTOR CORP. V. MAXIM INTEGRATED PRODS., INC. (2015) 236 CAL.APP.4TH 243, 254.) THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT'S CCP SECTION 128.7(B) MOTION FOR SANCTIONS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES THIS DATE FOR FULL RULING.) DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302)

calendar.matters:
Defendant Amy Tuckers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Cal. Civ. Code §3426.4?
Off Calendar Oct-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: SHAHRAM NASSI, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel No. 415-438-5907 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. GLENN M. GOFFIN, ESQ., Tel No. 415-845-8556 appearing for Defendant Amy Tucker.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMY TUCKER'S Defendant Amy Tuckers Motion For Attorneys Fees Pursuant To Cal. Civ. Code Sec. 3426.4 is granted in the amount of $50,000. Defendant is entitled to fees pursuant to California Civil Code section 3426.4 because Defendant was the prevailing party in the matter brought by Plaintiff and Plaintiff's claim was made in bad faith. (Cypress Semiconductor Corp. v. Maxim Integrated Prods., Inc. (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 243, 254.) The court granted Defendant's CCP section 128.7(b) motion for sanctions. In granting the order, the court found that there was no evidence to support Plaintiff's claims and that Plaintiff pursued the case for an improper purpose. The court's findings indicate that Defendant is the prevailing party on a "practical level." (Cypress Semiconductor Corp. v. Maxim Integrated Prods., Inc. (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 243, 254-55.) Defendant has also satisfied the bad faith requirement that the claim be objectively specious and made in subjective bad faith. (See Gemini Aluminum Corp. v. California Custom Shapes, Inc. (2002) 95 Cal.AApp.4th 1249.) The fact that the court found Plaintiff's claim to be frivolous means that the claim was also objectively specious. The subjective bad faith element is satisfied by the court's finding that the case was brought for the improper purpose of retaliating against Defendant for filing a wage claim. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that a lodestar multiplier is applicable in this case. Based on the nature of the case, the fact that the case was dismissed after four months, the vagueness of the billing descriptions. and the fact that Defendant already recovered attorney fees in bringing the sanctions motion, an appropriate attorney fee award in this case is $50,000. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
